name: a spec's dependency hash changes when the function it directly references changes, invalidating its cache entry
command:
mkdir -p r/rubric/runners r/rubric/detectors r/generated-code-quality-standards/x s && printf 'echo A; bash "$PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/runners/toolB.sh"
' > r/rubric/runners/toolA.sh && printf 'echo B
' > r/rubric/runners/toolB.sh && printf 'echo unrelated
' > r/rubric/detectors/unrelated.sh && printf 'echo enforce; reads generated-code-quality-standards rules
' > r/rubric/runners/enforceX.sh && printf 'id: r1
' > r/generated-code-quality-standards/x/r.yaml && printf '%s' '{"command":"x $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/runners/toolA.sh"}' > s/unit-a.json && printf '%s' '{"command":"x $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/runners/enforceX.sh"}' > s/enforce.json && printf '%s' '{"command":"echo hi with no substrate ref"}' > s/noref.json && node "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/evals/dep-hash.js" r s od 2>/dev/null; g(){ cat "od/$1" 2>/dev/null; }; h0=$(g unit-a); printf 'echo A changed
' > r/rubric/runners/toolA.sh; node "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/evals/dep-hash.js" r s od2 2>/dev/null; h1=$(cat od2/unit-a 2>/dev/null); [ "$h0" != "$h1" ] && echo invalidates-on-dep-change 1>&2
